response.justification: Wei Chen is an experienced full-stack and web-oriented developer with a broad polyglot background, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position mandates native-level Japanese proficiency — the candidate lists only English, Romanian, and German. The role requires deep C programming for embedded firmware and Linux kernel internals; the candidate's primary experience is TypeScript, Node.js, and web stacks, with only superficial C/C++ exposure at National Instruments in 2014–2015. There is no evidence of networking security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), switch chip familiarity, or NETCONF/SNMP work. These are hard disqualifiers, not gaps that can be bridged in onboarding.
strengths: Long career with embedded and low-latency systems exposure (D-lang async I/O at Sociomantic, C/C++ at National Instruments, hardware dev at Altom)
Strong Linux familiarity across multiple roles and personal projects, including kernel-adjacent tooling
Demonstrated ability to learn and deliver across diverse stacks and domains quickly
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard, listed requirement that makes the candidate ineligible
No security domain experience whatsoever: zero exposure to MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, or any network-security feature area
Embedded C / Linux kernel source modification experience is minimal and over a decade old; current work is entirely web and AI tooling

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
